strand_description: The GAS strand is a flexible academic track that exposes students to a broad range of subjects across multiple disciplines — sciences, humanities, business, and social sciences. It does not specialize in a single domain and is suited for students who are still exploring their interests or who need cross-disciplinary exposure.
holland_rationale: GAS students do not have a single dominant Holland profile due to the generalist nature of the strand. Social is common because the strand's humanities and social science exposure attracts interpersonally-oriented students. Conventional appears in students who chose GAS for its structured and manageable curriculum. The actual RIASEC type of a GAS student is best determined from their individual responses rather than strand alone — the LLM should weight personal signals more heavily than strand for GAS students.
scct_typical_outcome_expectations: Various — highly individual for GAS students
grade_note: GAS students have highly variable academic profiles. Program eligibility should be assessed primarily on GWA, individual subject performance, and personal signals from the assessment — not strand alone. GAS students should never be filtered out of a program solely on strand basis if their personal profile is strong.
special_handling_instruction: For GAS students, the Stage 1 LLM should place HIGHER weight on individual question responses (especially q4, q6, and q7) and LOWER weight on strand-based assumptions when determining RIASEC type and SCCT signals. GAS is the most heterogeneous strand.
common_student_profiles: The Undecided Explorer — not yet sure of career direction, benefits most from RIASEC/SCCT analysis
The Pragmatic Student — chose GAS for flexibility, has hidden strengths to be surfaced through assessment
The People Person — drifted to GAS from HUMSS interest, Social type likely dominant
The Business-Curious — interested in commerce but did not enter ABM, Enterprising/Conventional signals likely
